item.title: Jaylen Brown reportedly traded from Celtics to 76ers
item.description: Jaylen Brown is done with the Boston Celtics. The Celtics have traded Brown to Philadelphia 76ers.

item.title: U.S. takes down Bosnia 2-0 for second-ever World Cup knockout round win
item.description: Folarin Balogun got the scoring going with a goal in the 45th minute, but was sent off with a controversial red card in the 64th minute.

item.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
item.description: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angelina Nikolau, 33.

item.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers in custody after apparently getting engaged at the top
item.description: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Trump lauds "very good meetings" as indirect U.S.-Iran talks resume
item.description: Vance says the U.S. has accomplished its "core mission" in Iran, as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner meet mediators in Qatar, but not Iranians.

item.title: FBI investigating legitimacy of Nancy Guthrie ransom notes
item.description: The FBI said in a statement Wednesday that some ransom notes in Nancy Guthrie's disappearance have been "deemed to be extortion attempts without legitimacy," and other "demands may potentially be legitimate and are still being investigated as such."

item.title: See the full U.S. men's soccer schedule for the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team kicked off its 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium on Friday.

item.title: Coast Guard rescue swimmer whose first mission was Camp Mystic recalls tragedy: "Life-changing day"
item.description: It's been almost a year since deadly floods killed more than 130 people in the Hill Country region of Central Texas. The flooding at Camp Mystic killed 25 campers, two counselors and the camp's executive director. Scott Ruskan, aviation survival technician 2nd class for the U.S. Coast Guard, joined CBS News to remember the tragedy, his first ever mission.

item.title: Child psychologist breaks down the risks of ZYN and other nicotine pouches for young people
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration now allows ZYN nicotine pouches to be marketed as less harmful than cigarettes. Child psychologist Bonnie Halpern-Felsher joins "The Daily Report" to discuss the risks.

item.title: Breaking down the Supreme Court decision to lift limits on political party spending
item.description: The Supreme Court has lifted limits on how much money political parties can spend on congressional campaigns ahead of the midterms. In a 6-3 vote, the justices found the Federal Election Campaign Act violated the First Amendment. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: Why democratic socialists are racking up primary wins
item.description: Democratic socialists are celebrating another win in a key U.S. congressional primary ahead of the 2026 midterms. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: Harvard professor known for alien theories to lead White House UFO council
item.description: Avi Loeb, a Harvard astronomer who has made headlines with his theories about alien encounters, will lead a White House scientific advisory council on UFOs.

item.title: EMS was called to Mitch McConnell's home for "unconscious" patient last month
item.description: On the same morning Sen. Mitch McConnell was hospitalized last month, EMS personnel went to his home to respond to an unconscious person who appeared to experience "cardiac arrest," according to a dispatch call.

item.title: Fraunces Tavern: Where Americans can party like a founding father
item.description: If you want to party like a Founding Father, Fraunces Tavern in New York City promises that experience this July 4. The owner says the Sons of Liberty drank, argued and celebrated there. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: NCAA chief: No plan to change rules on transgender athletes after SCOTUS ruling
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told CBS News he doesn't think the group will need to change its rules on transgender athletes in light of a Supreme Court ruling that allowed states to ban their participation.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: Trump takes his first ride aboard a luxury new Air Force One gifted by Qataris
item.description: President Trump responded to new ethics questions on two fronts - the use of a luxury Boeing Jet, gifted by the Qatari government as a temporary Air Force One, and his newly disclosed billion-dollar crypto fortune. Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: Historian Jon Meacham on the national mood surrounding America 250
item.description: Historian Jon Meacham is serving as the National Constitution Center's semiquincentennial scholar for the nation's 250th birthday. Meacham joins "The Takeout" to discuss the festivities and how Americans are feeling about the anniversary.

item.title: 7/1: CBS Evening News
item.description: Empire State Building climbers engaged and under arrest; roads and airports packed with millions of July Fourth travelers.

item.title: H. R. McMaster says Iran is "getting everything that they wanted" in U.S. talks
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday touted progress in negotiations between the U.S. and Iran. Former Trump national security adviser H. R. McMaster joins with analysis.

item.title: About 2,000 U.S. troops supporting relief efforts after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Almost 60,000 buildings may have been damaged or destroyed in Venezuela after two powerful earthquakes last week, according to a NASA satellite assessment.

item.title: 7/1: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump filing reveals he made $2.2 billion in 2025; H. R. McMaster says Iran is "getting everything that they wanted" in U.S. talks.

item.title: U.S. says it won't extend key trade deal with Canada and Mexico
item.description: The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement will remain in effect until it expires in 2036, unless the countries strike another deal to extend it.

item.title: U.S. helicopter goes down in Arabian Sea, crew member missing, Navy says
item.description: The helicopter, which was assigned to the USS George H.W. Bush, is not believed to have been taken down by hostile action, the Navy said.

item.title: Sony to stop making physical discs for PlayStation starting in 2028
item.description: After January 2028, new games will be available exclusively in digital format from the PlayStation Store and at retailers, Sony said.

item.title: Victor Willis, Village People lead singer, dies at 74
item.description: Victor Willis, lead singer of the disco group Village People, whose hit "Y.M.C.A." became a fixture at rallies for President Trump, has died at the age of 74, his wife and the band said.

item.title: Actor Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer's diagnosis
item.description: Actor Danny Glover spoke about his Alzheimer's diagnosis in an interview with NBC's "Today" show, revealing that he has been living with the disease for several years.

item.title: Labor activist Dolores Huerta says Trump "does not know history"
item.description: Longtime labor activist Dolores Huerta says President Trump's disparaging remarks about Mexicans show he "does not know history," and called on Latinos in California, Texas and other states to push for change.

item.title: Egg producers reach federal and state settlement after price-fixing probe
item.description: Three U.S. egg producers will be required to provide 53 million eggs to food banks and to pay a $3.3 million financial penalty.
